(kips)
(ft-kip)
Weight of Concrete Cap
Load Combinations
Note: Number of cycles for earthquake loading conditions are 6 and 10 for Level-1 (M 5.0) and Level-2 (M 6.5) EQ,
respectively (Tokimatsu and Seed, 1987)
Note:  The model for Anchor Block Nos. 30, 31 and 33 were the actual size of the deadman anchor.
Note: The model for Anchor Block No. 32 was simplified by taking a 24-foot section of the deadman with 8
compression piles and 12 tension piles to simulate the 106-foot long deadman anchor.
Loads in kips
Based on information provided by the project structural engineer, the following loads were considered in the anchor
block pile group lateral load analyses. The positive directions of the coordinate system used are shown in the
following Pile Group Geometry Diagram.

2 Pile Group
Pile cap is simulated to take into account passive earth pressure resistance.
Global coordinate system is set at the center of the concrete cap at top of the cap.
HP14X89 piles are selected in various batter inclinations (as follows) spaced at 2 to 4 feet center to center.
Top of pile is modeled at bottom of cap.  Pile top and tip elevations are summarized as follows.

APPENDIX  A 
Pile Axial Compressive Load Capacity Analyses 
FY06 MCON Project P-431
Alpha and Bravo Wharf Improvements
Polaris Point, Guam

Steel HP14×89

PILE AXIAL COMPRESSIVE LOAD CAPACITY ANALYSES
H-pile may behave differently under static and dynamic loading conditions.  Under static loads, the soil between the flanges (plug) moves with the 
pile and the end bearing component may be utilizing the full pile cross section (Af).  However, the soil between the flanges may be slipped under 
dynamic loads and the end bearing component may only utilize the cross section of H-pile (As). 
The project site is underlain by granular fill, on-site non-liquefiable sandy soil and thicker liquefiable sandy soil over relative dense limestone 
formation.  It should be noted that on-site liquefiable material may behave as clayey soil under dynamic loading condition, while it behave as sandy 
soil under static loading condition.
(during dynamic loading conditions, on-site liquefiable soil will behave 
as soft clay with zero shear strength

APPENDIX  B 
Corrosion Analyses 
FY06 MCON Project P-431
Alpha and Bravo Wharf Improvements
Polaris Point, Guam
Using HP14×89 with thickness of 5/8" (web and flanges)
According to "FHWA-RD-89-189, Durability / Corrosion of Soil Reinforced Structures", 
Carbon steel corrosion rate, v (mm per year), equal to
v1 =
0.080
mm/yr
(during 1st and 2nd years)
v2 =
0.012
mm/yr
(thereafter)
Design Life =
50
years
Within the design life under aggressive corrosion environment
Loss of steel = 
0.74 mm
0.03 inch
Within the design life under very aggressive corrosion environment
Loss of steel = 
0.92 mm
0.04 inch
Within the design life under highly aggressive corrosion environment
Loss of steel = 
1.15 mm
0.05 inch
Say
1/16 inch
STEEL H- PILE CORROSION ANALYSES
0.08×2 + 0.012×(Life-2) =
1.25×[0.08×2 + 0.012×(Life-2)] =
1.25×1.25×[0.08×2 + 0.012×(Life-2)]=

     * TABLE I *   COMPUTATION ON INDIVIDUAL PILE
       * PILE GROUP *  1
 PILE TOP DISPLACEMENTS AND REACTIONS
